MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. GREGORY M. SHEFFER, Tel No. 510-853-5260 appearing for the Plaintiffs Sean Nicholson, and Shawn Nicholson;
Atty. SCOTT BRUST of Daniel Crowley & Associates, Tel No. (707) 525-8999 appearing for the Defendant House of Air, LLC.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court modified its tentative ruling regarding PLAINTIFFS SEAN NICHOLSON AND SHAWN NICHOLSON'S Motion To Compel Further Responses From Defendant To First Set Of Interrogatories and First Set Of Requests For Production Of Documents and Request For Monetary Sanctions, and ruled as follows: Plaintiff's Motion to Compel further response from HOA to First Set Request for Production of Document No. 11 is granted. The Court does not find the request to be overbroad. The safety measures are to be produced. The Motion is granted in part for Document No. 25. Defendant represents it has not attempted to contact any customers present on December 30, 2012. On the condition that HOA refrains from initiating private contact with such customers in the future, a letter shall be mailed out by Plaintiffs' counsel on HOA letterhead to all customers present at HOA on December 30, 2012 from the time period prior to the ambulance departing from HOA. The letter will come with a neutral response postcard to be mailed back to Plaintiffs' counsel indicating whether the customer saw the incident or not. The parties shall meet and confer on the form of this letter. Plaintiffs' counsel shall provide any customer response information to defense counsel first prior to contacting the customer further. Plaintiff's Motion is granted as to Document No. 38, subject to the parties' stipulated confidentiality provisions. The Motion is denied as to Document No. 39 on the grounds that the Court sustains Defendant's overbroad objection. Plaintiff's request for monetary sanctions is denied. PLAINTIFFS' COUNSEL TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Connie J. Parchman, CSR #6137, Tel No. (510) 853-5260 (302/EHG)

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-12-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
CROSS-DEFENDANT SHAWN NICHOLSON's DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT BY HOUSE OF AIR, LLC is sustained in part and overruled in part. (CCP 430.10(e) and (f).) The demurrer is sustained with 20 days leave to amend as to the 1st Cause of Action ("COA") for equitable indemnity. The Cross-Complaint ("X-C") does not adequately plead facts establishing that Cross-Defendant is jointly and severally liable to Plaintiff. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the 2nd COA for contribution as unopposed. The demurrer is sustained with 20 days leave to amend as to the 3rd COA for declaratory relief for Cross-Complainant to plead facts clarifying how the alleged indemnity arises; i.e.: either by virtue of contract or under equitable principles. The demurrer is sustained with 20 days leave to amend as to the 4th COA for breach of contract. Cross-Complainant may be able to state a COA that Cross-Defendant breached Exhibit B. The demurrer is sustained with 20 days leave to amend as to the 5th COA for express indemnity. As pled, it is unclear how Cross-Complainant is entitled to express indemnity. The demurrer to the 6th COA for intentional fraud is overruled. The X-C adequately pleads the elements of fraud. The demurrer to the 7th COA for negligent misrepresentation is overruled. The X-C adequately pleads the elements of this COA. The demurrer to the 8th and 9th COAs for intentional and negligent interference with prospective economic relations is overruled. Liberally construing the X-C, as the Court must on demurrer, the X-C adequately pleads the elements of these COAs, including an existing economic relationship. The Court did not consider the Reply Declaration of Gregory M. Sheffer in ruling on this demurrer. A demurrer may only be used to challenge defects that appear on the face of the pleading or from matters judicially noticeable. (Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318.) (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: A. James Robertson II; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR # 11185 =302/JR
